posts.title: The Harness Is Doing More Work Than You Think
posts.excerpt: Notes from moving a flaky multi-repo agent workflow out of the prompt and into the harness. Branch fallbacks, repo-specific startup conventions, and non-standard layouts, all encoded as structure instead of left for the model to re-infer on every run. A prompt describes intent. A runbook makes execution repeatable, and the returns on tightening the harness are larger than the returns on a better prompt or a fancier model.

posts.title: Building a Clipboard Manager for macOS
posts.excerpt: I recently switched from Windows to macOS and missed the built-in clipboard history (Win+V). Built a browser-based clipboard manager using IndexedDB - stores text and images locally with no frameworks or backend. Chose IndexedDB over localStorage for better performance with large images and unlimited storage. The tool supports pasting anywhere on the page (Cmd/Ctrl+V) and stores everything client-side with no auto-deletion.
